problem: Chats incorrectly treated visible native connectors as the complete capability boundary.
root_cause: Capability facts existed across repo workflows, BoCore, self-hosted runner, secret-backed workflows and prior transfer packages, but were not canonicalized into a mandatory chat bootstrap.
solution: Added owner-resource authorization, canonical software/runtime inventory and mandatory capability bootstrap. Future chats must check indirect governed paths before declaring a capability unavailable.
next_action: Keep bootstrap and authorization contract updated whenever capability drift is discovered.

problem: PR313 File Dispatch Bridge triggered on unrelated pull requests to main and blocked governance PRs on the self-hosted runner lane.
root_cause: The workflow used a broad pull_request trigger without a paths filter even though the job was PR313-specific.
solution: Scoped the pull_request trigger to .adcp/live-dispatch/pr313-dispatch-request.json and kept PR313 logic specific to PR313.
next_action: Do not add specialized workflow bridges without exact path filters or explicit gate logic that prevents unrelated PR scheduling.

problem: BoCore self-hosted Linux runner can see Windows filesystem and WSL_INTEROP, but direct execution of Windows .exe files such as powershell.exe and cmd.exe from the runner job fails with Invalid argument.
root_cause: Runner process is a WSL/Linux GitHub Actions service context where Windows PE/bimfmt interop is not executable from the job despite visible /mnt/c paths and WSL_INTEROP socket evidence.
solution: Use a Windows-side governed service channel or the Windows-native extended runner lane instead of direct WSL-to-Windows .exe execution.
next_action: Prefer github-actions-runner-03-windows or the authenticated Windows-side service channel for Windows-native operations.

problem: GitHub Projects V2 capability was previously unclear from direct chat connector inventory.
root_cause: Direct connector did not expose all GraphQL Projects V2 mutation actions, but secret-backed governed workflows had the required access.
solution: Recorded GitHub Projects V2 as proven through ELEMENTARY_GITHUB_PROJECTS_TOKEN governed workflows; created/linked canonical projects Platform V1, Studio V1 and Node V1.
next_action: Use governed workflows for Projects V2 mutations and never expose token values.

problem: Production cPanel environment cannot be assumed to have Node/npm/npx or root/systemd services.
root_cause: Production is shared cPanel/CloudLinux/CageFS on SuperHosting, not VPS/root runtime.
solution: Canonical production runtime records PHP 8.5.6, Composer 2.9.8, Python 3.6.8, MariaDB 10.6.27 and Node/npm/npx unavailable on production.
next_action: Do not design production workflows requiring Node/npm on the cPanel server; use build artifacts or external/BoCore runner paths.

problem: BoCore had one self-hosted Linux runner lane with practical concurrency 1, causing unrelated workflows to queue behind long jobs.
evidence: Runner 02 local registration completed and drained #554 queue
root_cause: Single self-hosted runner lane was used for validation, browser, BoCore, control-plane and bridge jobs.
solution: Added governed lane split: github-actions-runner-02 for WSL/Linux capacity relief and github-actions-runner-03-windows for Windows-native extended operations. PR313 over-triggers were also guarded so they do not occupy the lane on unrelated PRs.
next_action: Use explicit labels for new jobs: elementary-ci-fast for fast Linux validations and elementary-bocore-extended for Windows-native tasks. Keep ADCP as the only control plane.

problem: Attempted migration of read-only PR validation jobs to GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest produced instant job failures before any steps were available through the connector.
evidence: BoCore canonical software inventory run 29142177914 job 86517319423
root_cause: GitHub-hosted runner lane is not usable through the current repository/app/plan configuration or fails before step execution; exact platform reason requires GitHub UI/admin billing/runner settings verification.
solution: Reverted these validations to the existing self-hosted elementary-ci lane and removed PR313 over-trigger from unrelated PRs. Treat GitHub-hosted as unavailable until explicitly verified.
next_action: Use self-hosted lane separation or verify GitHub-hosted runner availability from repository settings before migrating validation jobs again.

problem: github-actions-runner-03-windows initially depended on an open foreground PowerShell window because svc.cmd was unavailable in the extracted runner package directory.
evidence: User local readback 2026-07-11: RUNNER03_BACKGROUND_TASK=PASS, TASK_STATE=Running, LAST_TASK_RESULT=267009, log shows Listening for Jobs at 2026-07-11 07:18:46Z
root_cause: The Windows runner package/config state contained .runner and run.cmd but did not provide svc.cmd for normal Windows service installation in this local setup.
solution: Registered a SYSTEM scheduled task at \ELEMENTARY\GitHub Runner 03 Windows that starts C:\actions-runner-03-windows\run.cmd through a wrapper and writes logs to C:\ELEMENTARY\LocalRunner\logs\runner03-scheduled.log. Task state is Running and runner log reports Listening for Jobs.
next_action: Keep scheduled task mode as accepted background operation unless a later service-mode repair proves svc.cmd/runsvc.cmd installation. Do not require the foreground PowerShell window once scheduled task mode is running.
